canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, LIAR is a dataset used for evaluating claim verification. The single source paper, "MultiSocial: Multilingual Benchmark of Machine-Generated Text Detection of Social-Media Texts", operationalizes this task as true/false classification. Performance on the dataset is assessed using the F1 score, with the paper noting an instance of "improving F1 score by 10.1 on LIAR dataset". This source presents LIAR as one of several "standard datasets" used for evaluation.
papers: Ask-Before-Detection: Identifying and Mitigating Conformity Bias inLLM-Powered Error Detector for Math Word Problem Solutions
Bregman Conditional Random Fields: Sequence Labeling with Parallelizable Inference Algorithms
MultiSocial: Multilingual Benchmark of Machine-Generated Text Detection of Social-Media Texts

definitions.text: Dataset for evaluating claim verification, used here to assess F1 score improvements in true/false classification under prompt optimization.
definitions.paper: MultiSocial: Multilingual Benchmark of Machine-Generated Text Detection of Social-Media Texts
